studyName: Regulatory and Effector T cells in Oral Immunotherapy for Food Allergy

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, affection status of the subject for peanut allergy, age, gender, race, cumulative eliciting dose, skin prick test, peanut-specific IgE titer in plasma, and clinical outcome of treatment with peanut oral immunotherapy.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Peanut allergy phenotype (Reactive or Hyporeactive; Reactive patients received peanut oral immunotherapy)

variables.name: CLINICAL_OUTCOME_OF_TREATMENT
variables.description: Clinical outcome of treatment with peanut oral immunotherapy

variables.name: CUMULATIVE_ELICITING_DOSE
variables.description: Cumulative eliciting dose (mg peanut protein) at first objective allergic reaction during baseline double-blind placebo-controlled food challenge

variables.name: SKIN_PRICK
variables.description: Skin prick test wheal size (mm) in response to peanut allergen

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site where sample was collected, analyte type, histological type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, and pooled subjects.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: POOLING
variables.description: Samples from these individual subjects are pooled in the corresponding pooled subject

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. This data table also includes pooled sample IDs.
